text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: The details are not the details. They make the design.
author: Charles Eames

text: Wabi-sabi is the beauty of things imperfect, impermanent, and incomplete.
author: Leonard Koren

text: Less, but better.
author: Dieter Rams

text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
author: Ram Dass

text: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities, in the expert's mind there are few.
author: Shunryu Suzuki

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Have nothing in your houses that you do not know to be useful or believe to be beautiful.
author: William Morris

text: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: The most valuable thing you can make is a mistake — you can't learn anything from being perfect.
author: Adam Osborne

text: Simplicity is not the goal. It is the by-product of a good idea and modest expectations.
author: Paul Rand

text: Empty your mind, be formless, shapeless — like water.
author: Bruce Lee

text: The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Beauty is the promise of happiness.
author: Stendhal

text: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: To see a world in a grain of sand, and heaven in a wild flower.
author: William Blake

text: Everything should be made as simple as possible, but not simpler.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The material is only an occasion for composition.
author: Henri Matisse

text: Form follows function — that has been misunderstood. Form and function should be one.
author: Frank Lloyd Wright

text: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: What you do not want done to yourself, do not do to others.
author: Confucius

text: The obstacle is the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu
